2010–2016: 12.8 (−4.7 to 21.9)

2017–2022: −0.9 (−11.6 to 4.2) 
Abbreviations: AAPC = average annual percent change; APC = annual percent change; CIN = cervical intraepithelial neoplasia; HPV-IMPACT = Human Papillomavirus Vaccine Impact Monitoring Project.
* Denominators are the estimated number of screened women aged 20–24, 25–29, 30–34, 35–39, 40–49, or 50–64 years living in the catchment areas.
† HPV-IMPACT sites are Alameda County, California; New Haven County, Connecticut; Monroe County, New York; Davidson County, Tennessee; and 28 zip codes in metropolitan Portland, Oregon.
§ CIN2+ includes grades 2 or worse and adenocarcinoma in situ.
¶ CIN3+ includes grade 3 and adenocarcinoma in situ.
** Data from 2020 were excluded from trend analyses. Analyses were also performed with 2020 data included. Most AAPCs were similar (within approximately 0.3) and did not change statistical significance. The CIN2+ AAPC among age group 50–64 years increased from 5.2 to 6.5, and the increase in CIN3+ among age group 35–39 years was similar but not statistically significant.
